page: Amazon Web Services r8gd.8xlarge is a memory-optimized instance powered by AWS Graviton processors. It features 32 dedicated physical cores on the arm64 architecture, utilizing the AWS ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 CPU model operating at 2.8 GHz. The processor includes 64 KB of L1d cache, 2 MB of L2 cache, and 36 MB of L3 cache, with hardware support for AES cryptography. The instance is equipped with 256.0 GB of system memory, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. It also includes 1900 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 15 Gbps, running on the AWS Nitro hypervisor.

Benchmark data highlights strong memory performance and multi-core efficiency. The instance achieves top-tier status in memory bandwidth tests, recording 405.8 GB/sec for large-block read operations and 184.15 GB/sec for large-block write operations. Passmark memory and database operations benchmarks also rank in the top tier, with database operations scoring 47362.41. Multi-core performance is robust, as evidenced by a top-tier Geekbench multi-core score of 21506.0. Single-core performance is strong across several tests, including Geekbench single-core and software compilation. However, certain workloads like large-model LLM prompt processing with a 70B model show weaker relative performance at 5.88 tokens/sec.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the r8gd.8xlarge offers high resource density by combining a substantial memory footprint with fast local NVMe SSD storage on an energy-efficient ARM architecture. This configuration is highly suitable for memory-intensive and database-driven workloads. The high memory bandwidth and strong database operation scores make it an appropriate choice for hosting large-scale databases, Redis in-memory data stores, data caching layers, and high-throughput web serving. It is also well-suited for software compilation and multi-threaded data decompression tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services r8gd.8xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 32 dedicated physical cores powered by the 2.8 GHz AWS ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 processor. Built on the arm64 architecture and managed by the Nitro hypervisor, it delivers 256.0 GB of system memory alongside 1900 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and 15 Gbps of baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark results indicate top-tier memory performance, with large-block read bandwidth reaching 405.8 GB/sec and database operations scoring exceptionally high. The instance also demonstrates strong multi-core capabilities, achieving a Geekbench multi-core score of 21506.0. This hardware profile is highly efficient for memory-intensive applications, making it suitable for large-scale databases, Redis deployments, high-throughput web serving, and software compilation workloads.

tagline: An arm64 memory-optimized instance featuring dedicated ARMv9 cores, high-speed local NVMe storage, and top-tier memory bandwidth for database workloads.

256.0 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core.
